purpose: Session-local baseline regime. The unit of evidence is the admitted session. No globally portable baseline exists.

version_history.v2: Original: batch stabilization (all-A then all-B), max_delta <= 0.001.
version_history.v2.1: 2026-03-17: Per-probe 3-run modal stabilization. Motivated by rs-ownership structure-score boundary oscillation and js-generics KV cache interference. See pre_day4_addendum.md for full rationale.

epistemology.claim_shape: Within an admitted warm session, the frozen intervention produces paired pre/post gains on the fit set, improvement on the eligible holdout subset, and no regression on sentinel holdouts.
epistemology.not_claimed: The system improved by X against a canonical baseline across the month.
epistemology.unit_of_analysis: The admitted session, not the repo commit and not the design baseline.
epistemology.design_baseline_role: Diagnostic artifact only (commit 4b4b083). Not used for pass/fail. Logged for drift monitoring.

cold_start_invariant.description: Server MUST be freshly started before session admission. No prior inference calls from any source.
cold_start_invariant.procedure: pkill -9 llama-server; sleep 2; start server in tmux with frozen flags; wait for health ok; then run session_admit.py
cold_start_invariant.rationale: Day 2-3 showed that servers with prior inference history produce different stabilization behavior. The exact mechanism is not fully characterized. Campaign controls for it by requiring a known initial state.
cold_start_invariant.scope: Results are valid only under this cold-start regime. They do not claim generalizability to long-running or warm-state servers.

warmup_procedure.step_1: After server reports healthy (/health returns ok), run 3 throwaway generic inference calls (discarded). Flushes CUDA initialization.
warmup_procedure.step_1b: Run 1 throwaway round of all 5 anchor probes (results discarded). Primes KV cache with probe-like content.
warmup_procedure.step_2: Wait 3 seconds for GPU state to stabilize.
warmup_procedure.step_3: For each anchor probe individually: run 3 consecutive times with frozen parameters (temp=0, seed=42, top_k=1, max_tokens=2048).
warmup_procedure.step_4: For each probe, check 2-of-3 modal agreement (scores within 0.001). If any probe has no 2-of-3 agreement, the session has not stabilized — repeat steps 1-4 up to 2 more times. The modal score is used as the stabilized value.
warmup_procedure.step_5: If all 5 probes have modal agreement, session is internally stable. Proceed to admission.
warmup_procedure.step_4_rationale: Changed from batch 2-run exact to per-probe 3-run modal in v2.1. Batch runs caused KV cache interference (js-generics) and masked per-probe structure-score oscillation (rs-ownership). Per-probe isolation + modal voting detects the most common output without suppressing genuine drift.

admission_check.description: Admission proves intra-session determinism. It does NOT compare to a fixed reference.
hard_fail_rules: Stabilization failed after 3 warmup cycles → SESSION REJECTED. Server is non-deterministic.
Any anchor returns empty response → SESSION REJECTED. Server malfunction.
admission_check.if_rejected: Do not proceed. Investigate server state, restart, retry.
admission_check.if_admitted: The stabilized anchor scores become this session's local baseline. Proceed to full baseline capture.

session_baseline_capture.description: After admission, run ALL 40 campaign probes once. This is the session's pre-intervention baseline.
session_baseline_capture.probes: All probes in campaign_full_baseline.py (JS=10, Python=10, Rust=10, C++=10).
session_baseline_capture.timing: Must complete before any training attempt in this session.
session_baseline_capture.role_labels_computed: weakness_type and holdout_role are computed from session-local scores, not from the design baseline.

diagnostic_comparison.description: After baseline capture, compare session-local scores to the design baseline (commit 4b4b083). This is informational only — it does not gate anything.
diagnostic_comparison.action: Log only. Do not reject session. Do not modify campaign design.

non_anchor_sentinels.purpose: Spot-check non-anchor fit probes to verify broader stability beyond anchors.
non_anchor_sentinels.recheck_rule: Run all 4 sentinels before each counted campaign attempt. Compare to session baseline (not design baseline). If any sentinel deviates > tolerance from its session baseline score, the attempt is PAUSED and anchors are re-verified. If anchors also drifted, the epoch is INVALIDATED.

failure_disposition.in_flight_attempt: DISCARD. The attempt was started under uncertain measurement state.
failure_disposition.most_recent_completed_attempt: VALID if it preceded the last passing sentinel check. QUARANTINED if sentinel check was not run between it and the failing check.
failure_disposition.artifacts_since_last_pass: QUARANTINED until re-admission. If re-admitted session produces matching sentinel scores, quarantined results may be reinstated. Otherwise discarded.
failure_disposition.rule: strict-with-quarantine: nothing after the last clean check counts until re-admission resolves the ambiguity.

non_anchor_sentinels.rationale: Anchors alone are not proven sufficient to predict broader probe stability. Sentinels catch intra-session drift that anchors might miss.

epoch_validity.description: An admitted session (measurement epoch) remains valid until an invalidation event occurs. This section defines those events explicitly.
valid_until: failed sentinel re-check (sentinel drifted AND anchors confirm drift)
not_invalidated_by: number of attempts alone (no count-based expiry)
informational audit findings (only binding audit gates)
sentinel drift that anchors do not confirm (log warning, continue)
epoch_validity.on_invalidation: Stop all campaign work. Open a new epoch: re-warmup, re-admit, re-capture full baseline. Results from the invalidated epoch remain valid up to the last clean sentinel check. Results after the invalidating event are discarded.

hard_rules.rule_1_session_container: Each campaign run must have a session_id and a frozen session manifest. No result counts unless attached to an admitted session.
hard_rules.rule_2_paired_deltas_only: For every fit or holdout evaluation, compute effect against the pre-intervention baseline from the SAME admitted session. Never compare to the design baseline or any other session.
hard_rules.rule_3_no_cross_session_raw_scores: Raw scores across restarts are not comparable. Aggregate only on: closure/non-closure, signed delta, normalized headroom closed, regression/non-regression.
hard_rules.rule_4_restart_new_epoch: Any restart opens a new measurement epoch. New admission, new baseline, new session_id. Never splice pre-restart and post-restart raw scores into one series.
hard_rules.rule_5_sentinel_recheck: Run sentinel spot-check before every counted attempt. Sentinel drift + anchor confirmation = epoch invalidated.

reporting_rules.description: How results are reported. Raw delta alone is not sufficient because +0.10 from 0.90 closes all remaining headroom while +0.10 from 0.50 closes only part.
required_metrics_per_attempt: headroom_closed_fraction ((post - pre) / (1.0 - pre)) — normalized by available headroom)
reporting_rules.aggregation: Stratify by starting-score band (below 0.70, 0.70-0.90, above 0.90) when reporting across buckets. Do not mix high-headroom and low-headroom bucket deltas into one unstratified mean.
final_report_channels: Fit-phase: per-bucket raw delta + headroom-closed + closure binary
Improvement-sensitive holdout: same metrics, only eligible probes
Regression-sentinel holdout: regressed/stable only, no improvement claim

post_gate4_change_rules.description: After Gate 4 (dry-run) passes, only operational bug fixes are allowed.
allowed: Bug fixes to scripts that do not change measurement semantics
forbidden: Informational audit findings reshaping the dataset
post_gate4_change_rules.enforcement: After any post-Gate-4 fix, run an automatic sealed-artifact check proving no change to: canonical_buckets.json hash, split_manifest.json hash, canonical_counts.json hash, session_protocol.json semantics (protocol_version field), weakness_classifier_version. If any hash changed, the fix is not operational — it is a design change and must be escalated.

fields: warmup_rounds (how many cycles needed for stability)

campaign_limitation.statement: The measurement instrument is session-dependent. Cold-start drift up to 0.116 was observed. Evidence is valid only as paired within-session deltas under a controlled admission protocol.
campaign_limitation.containment: Session-local baselines with explicit admission gate.
campaign_limitation.post_campaign_target: Eliminate session-dependent variance (deterministic CUDA mode, fixed KV cache init, warm-start checkpoint).
